e7ccbe7c0e72e6d28db8eab7d7aa3c82ec2a6949.reply: Added Graduate Scenarios — SHL's situational judgement test designed specifically for graduate-level candidates. The previous three items are unchanged:

706715c2df6b27b6440637b444417c147f0f9e29.reply: Good two-stage design. That keeps the initial screen fast while reserving knowledge tests for candidates who've cleared the cognitive bar.

9feed0ecbd42dbb7bdd803734667ff07807ce117.reply: For a compact audit-and-development stack: **self-reported skills** (GSA plus its development report for re-skilling initiative), **personality** via OPQ32r, a **sales-specific OPQ view** with optional motivators, and **Sales Transformation** for how reps show up in a digital-first selling model. First-line sales managers can follow the same spine and use the Sales Manager Sales Transformation report from the catalog when you split leader vs. rep reporting.

1cc0cfdffbfd8317e673a7ac801fe7930e90987b.reply: **OPQ (OPQ32r)** is the underlying **personality questionnaire**: a broad, standard measure of workplace behavioural style used across roles and decisions (development, team fit, etc.). **OPQ MQ Sales Report** is a **reporting product**, not a different questionnaire. It summarizes OPQ results in a **sales-specific** way—graphical and narrative emphasis on behaviours tied to sales success. You can **optionally** add the **Motivation Questionnaire (MQ)** so the same report also reflects **sales motivators and drives**; without MQ, you still get the sales-framed OPQ story. So: one assessment instrument (OPQ32r) for personality; the Sales Report is how you **read** those results for sellers (and optionally enrich with MQ).

616e7ea017152447427353dc1d9d79ca624aa10d.reply: That matches how the catalog is built: OPQ32r once, OPQ MQ Sales Report for sales-language feedback and optional MQ, plus GSA and its development report for skills and associated re-skilling, and Sales Transformation 2.0 for rep-level digital selling behaviours.

a8877498da0bac97df03f79071f5b338a57428e9.reply: For a safety-critical frontline role where dependability and rule compliance are the primary concern, the assessment focus must be on personality predictors of safety behaviour — not just knowledge tests. A knowledge test tells you what someone knows about safety; a personality instrument predicts whether they'll actually follow through.

fbcfe786a3eb7ca7622e4e756526a8bbe1156dc1.reply: Both measure safety-relevant personality, but at different levels. The DSI is a standalone instrument measuring integrity, reliability, and safety attitudes — used across sectors. The Manufacturing & Industrial Safety & Dependability 8.0 is a sector-specific bundled solution with norms calibrated to manufacturing and industrial workforces. If your facility is industrial-classified, the 8.0 gives you industry norms. If you want a general standalone instrument, the DSI is the right choice.

92841b32e544f97bf54d6bdb3be4c049d2526f49.reply: Good choice for an industrial context. Shortlist confirmed.

82caa2f122c89a14ff5941a0df8ec4561b660116.reply: There's a real catalog constraint here: the role-specific knowledge tests for healthcare admin — HIPAA (Security), Medical Terminology, Microsoft Word — are English-only. The personality measures (OPQ32r, DSI) do support Latin American Spanish. Two ways to run this: (a) Hybrid: knowledge tests in English, personality in Spanish. Workable if your candidates are functionally bilingual at a working level for the English knowledge tests. (b) Personality-only in Spanish, and assess HIPAA/medical terminology through your own structured interview. Trades off measurement rigour for language fit. Which fits your candidate pool better?

f39fb74088a8fb7f2f5530d6b139d3a2aa1b970d.reply: For a trust-sensitive role with patient-record access, two complementary personality measures: DSI focuses specifically on dependability and safety-critical reliability — directly relevant to HIPAA-protected information handling. OPQ32r gives a broader workplace-behaviour profile. Both run in Latin American Spanish. Full hybrid battery:

b7a72225c108e513ecb98bf8a50278df73bf68b0.reply: Those are legal compliance questions outside what I can advise on — I can help you select assessments, but not interpret regulatory obligations or whether a specific test satisfies a legal requirement. Your legal or compliance team is the right resource for that. What I can confirm: HIPAA (Security) measures knowledge of HIPAA security provisions. Whether using it fulfils a particular regulatory obligation is a question for counsel.

cc5f508ad66525afe847085d5af346020ba62a20.reply: Confirmed. Hybrid battery as above — knowledge tests in English, DSI and OPQ32r in Latin American Spanish.

7f9f19d1cfef86d6931774041d39315c42f6b80c.reply: For a quick knowledge check, the knowledge-only variants are the right fit — they test conceptual understanding. We also have end-to-end simulations to test these capabilties but I have excluded them due to the time constraint. 53e4dfaa59a348bd40e53f4ef92dc8368842786e.reply: Yes. Advanced covers concurrency, JVM internals, performance tuning, and the kind of design-level Java that comes up when extending production microservices — which matches "owns end-to-end microservice delivery". The Entry-Level test is calibrated for graduate hires reading and modifying existing code, which undershoots a senior IC. Keeping Advanced.

5624303bb6b429c1d6a8af3f32bbf6badac85557.reply: Not redundant — they measure different things. The technical tests confirm the candidate already knows the stack. Verify G+ measures general reasoning ability, which predicts how quickly they'll learn parts of the stack they haven't seen and adapt when production throws something unfamiliar at them. For a senior IC making architectural calls, that signal matters. That said, if you want a leaner shortlist, dropping Verify G+ is a valid tradeoff and the battery still stands. Your call.

742ede93df75fab7235013e615f20cfe4be64aa8.reply: Final battery — Java Advanced, Spring, SQL, AWS, and Docker as the technical core; Verify G+ for reasoning; OPQ32r for personality fit on a senior IC who'll mentor and contribute to architecture.
